chunks.title: Benefits of Animal Fats
chunks.summary: The segment covers the nutritional benefits of animal fats, including fat-soluble vitamins and steric acid, explaining why the host prefers fattier meat over lean options.
chunks.transcript: strip grass-fed ribey they've got grass-fed 8515 ground beef that's the staple so I'm going to get a couple of ground beefs when I'm looking for meat I'm always looking for the fattiest cuts of meat I'm looking for the fattiest ground beef that I can get I don't fear animal fats traditionally in mainstream nutritional culture we've been told that animal fat is bad for you I think animal fat is some of the best healthiest food for you full of fat soluble nutrients like vitamin E vitamin K2 animal fats in general also contain steric acid and 18 carbon saturated fatty acid that's been shown in human studies to kind of affect mitochondria the energy plant in your cells positively to turn on fat burning in animal studies clearly leads to reduction of visceral fat when animals are given steric acid which is found in animal fat thus 8515 ground beef not 90 three seven I don't want the leaner ground beef I want the fattier ground

chunks.title: Avoiding Chicken and Processed Foods
chunks.summary: The host shares their opinions on chicken, stating a preference for red meat due to its nutritional density, while also expressing concerns about the quality of chicken feed and processed foods.
chunks.transcript: beef to mix it up I also like to get some lamb so I get some ground lamb I'll get some rib chops from lamb too let me talk to you guys about what I'm not getting from meat chicken is okay they've got organic chicken here but I'm not a huge fan of chicken I feel like red meat whether it's beef or bison or lamb is more nutritious more nutrient rich and it has more fat and the chicken that's here even though it's organic is fed corn and soy and that corn and soy leads to accumulation of linolic acid which is the thing I don't like in seed oils in the chicken fat chicken is an animal food it's better than processed garbage Foods it's just not my favorite

chunks.title: Pork Preferences
chunks.summary: Discussion on pork choices focuses on selecting grass-fed and pastured options to avoid high levels of linoleic acid, and the host expresses their cautious approach towards pork consumption.
chunks.transcript: choice of meat so when I have a choice I'm not going to get chicken I'm just going to avoid it I don't do stuff like this with sauces I'm not a fan of this kind of thing because if you look at the sauces I mean it looks like it's going to taste good but you read the ingredients and it's inevitably full of stuff that I don't want to eat this has soybean oil in the actual marinade I don't want to mess up my meat my meat is what's nutritious the organs are what's nutritious that's what I want and I'm going to season them with a good salt and I'll show you how I get a salt I'm also not a huge fan of the sausages they're better than garbage processed foods but the ingredients are just not great there's always stuff that sneaks into these bacon and pork it's delicious I eat it occasionally but when I do eat pork I try and get pork that's grass-fed grass finished or pastured just like chicken I'm not a huge fan of pork that's fed corn and soy because that corn and soy is high in polyunsaturated fats that linolic acid that accumulates in the pork fat it tastes good but I don't want large amounts of linolic acid in my diet so I generally steer away from pork unless I know that it's really really good pastured pork that is not fed corn and

chunks.title: Seasoning and Cooking Preferences
chunks.summary: The host describes their simplistic seasoning approach using only salt to enhance meat flavor, advocating for minimal interference in cooking to maintain the integrity of the meat's taste.
chunks.transcript: soy in terms of salt I like the texture of the Malon sea salt flakes that'll bring out the flavor in the meat how I season my meat I don't use pepper I just use salt I don't use a lot of other herbs and spices I just use salt cuz I want to taste the meat I actually had a chef tell me once that salt enhances the flavor of what you're cooking and pepper changes the flavor of what you're cooking I don't want to change the flavor of my meat I want to taste the meat so I just season it with salt and

chunks.title: Travel Tips for Maintaining Diet
chunks.summary: The host shares tips on managing their diet while traveling, including the convenience of bringing beef sticks made from quality ingredients, providing insight into maintaining healthy eating habits on the go.
